author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
gorā pa̐hu nā bhajiyā mainu
prema⋅ratana⋅dhana helāya hārāinu

adhane jatana kari’ dhana teyāginu
āpana karama⋅doṣe āpani ḍubinu

satsaṅga chāḍi’ kainu asate vilāsa
te⋅kāraṇe lāgila je karmabandha⋅phā̐sa

viṣaya viṣama viṣa satata khāinu
gaurakīrtana⋅rase magana nā hainu

kena vā āchaye prāṇa ki sukha pāiyā
narottama⋅dāsa kena nā gela mariyā
en_translation:
I died without worshiping Gaurāṅga Prabhu. I carelessly lost the treasure of the jewel of divine love.

Caring for what is worthless, I gave up true wealth. By my own faults, I have drowned myself.

Giving up the association of devotees, I indulged in bad company. For that reason, the noose of karmic bondage has caught me.

I constantly consumed the deadly poison of material enjoyment. I did not immerse myself in the nectar of chanting the glories of Gaura.

Why do I still have life? What happiness have I gained? Why did Narottama dāsa not simply die?
